human_verification.notes: Reviewed manifest, authority-source baseline, scenario suite, evaluation results, readiness review, deployment notes, and marketing notes for consistency with issue #141 and the repository delivery contract.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to workflow automation, approvals, queue and work-item orchestration, native connector guidance, automation governance, lifecycle readiness, troubleshooting, and platform operations.
Real public authoritative sources are documented with publisher, authority reason, review date, refresh interval, and decay policy in the manifest.

The package includes automation-specific scenarios for workflow design, queue quality, connector constraints, governance, ownership patterns, release readiness, monitoring, edge cases, out-of-scope rejection, low-confidence escalation, and shared-meta-agent fallback.
The manifest defines bootstrap metadata, delegated request and response contracts, and fallback behavior for shared meta-agents required by issue #141.
delegated_meta_agent_evidence: Best-practice corpus expansion is delegated to #225 with safe fallback to the local automation-platform source baseline.
Scenario normalization, readiness consistency checks, deployment packaging normalization, and commercialization wording review are delegated to #302 through #305 with local-artifact fallback.
Authority-source policy normalization, boundary-routing review, and scaffold consistency review are delegated to #306 through #308 without weakening the specialist-owned automation boundary.
notes: Structured evidence supports the validated claim required by issue #141.
Evaluation thresholds exceed the issue minimum with 12 scenarios and a 1.00 pass rate against a 0.92 threshold.
Deployment package documents runtime model, tenant isolation and adaptation constraints, rollback path, monitoring and logging expectations, and deferred shared-meta-agent initialization.
Marketing readiness notes document buyer profile, packaging, subscription model, proof points, and go-to-market gaps without widening scope.
